content.problemStatement:
Chef would like go shopping to buy ingredients for his special dish. The local grocery store has some special discount offers. If you want to buy some set of ingredients you will pay for all ingredients except the cheapest one. Chef would like to spend as little money as possible. You have to help him. :)



The store is pretty small and stocks only one unit of each ingredients. Opposite each ingredient is a hanging price tag corresponding to it. The salesman walked away for a minute, giving Chef an opportunity to swap some price tags. He would like to swap some tags to minimize his purchase cost.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines. The first line contains a single integer N denoting the number of ingredients Chef needs to buy. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the value written on the price tags opposite the needed ingredients. The third line contains a single integer M denoting the number of special offers. The following M lines list the inventory of special offers, one offer per line. Each line contains an integer Ci followed by Ci integers denoting the indices of ingredients constituting the ith discount offer.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimal purchase cost.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The minimum cost is achieved by buying the ingredients with prices 2, 3, and 4, and getting the ingredient with price 1 for free.
The minimum cost is achieved by buying the ingredients with prices 2 and 3, and getting the ingredient with price 1 for free.
The minimum cost is achieved by buying the ingredients with prices 1 and 2, and getting no ingredients for free.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy approach combined with bit manipulation to generate all possible subsets of the ingredients and calculate the cost for each subset. We start by generating all possible subsets of the ingredients. For each subset, we calculate the cost by summing up the prices of all ingredients in the subset except the cheapest one. We keep track of the minimum cost found so far. Finally, we output the minimum cost as the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 2^N * N) — The time complexity is dominated by the generation of all possible subsets of the ingredients and the calculation of the cost for each subset.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the prices of the ingredients and the current subset being processed.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    A.sort()
    M = int(input())
    minCost = float('inf')
    for mask in range(1 << N):
        cost = 0
        count = 0
        for i in range(N):
            if (mask & (1 << i)) != 0:
                cost += A[i]
                count += 1
        if count > 1:
            cost -= A[0]
        minCost = min(minCost, cost)
    print(minCost)
